channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for techraptor.net

item.title: New Dungeons & Dragons Villainous Options Unearthed Arcana Lets You Be The Bad Guy
item.content:encoded:
A new batch of playtest material is now live for Dungeons & Dragons. The new Dungeons & Dragons Villainous Options Unearthed Arcana provides a glimpse at new character subclasses and feats based around evil or villainous character concepts.

Like all prior playtest material, the Dungeons & Dragons Villainous Options Unearthed Arcana can be downloaded for free on DnD Beyond. The material contains a new subclass for the Druid, Cleric, Sorcerer, and Fighter classes, and ten new Feats.

As the name implies, these subclasses are all villain or evil-themed. The Circle of The Titan Druid enhances the use of Wild Shape with Titan Forms. These new forms grow the Druid to Huge or even Gargantuan size, letting them unleash their inner kaiju. 

Hell Knight Fighters are warriors that have fought for the devils of the Nine Hells, either through infernal pacts or as sellswords. This grants them the use of Hellfire weapons and a unique Infernal Wounds mechanic, which can inflict debilitating effects on those who stand in their way.

Sorcerers with Demonic Sorcery are those with blood ties to the demon princes of The Abyss. This allows the caster to conjure caustic ooze, mind-altering spores, or lashes of demonic energy. Since The Abyss is chaotic, however, these effects are randomized; determined by a d6 roll.

Lastly, the Pestilence Domain Cleric is all about creating supernatural plagues and decay. This is represented by their Channel Divinity causing levels of Exhaustion in an area-of-effect, causing defeated enemies to burst in a wave of disease, and the ability to shapeshift into a swarm of insects.

Curiously, the ten new feats in the Dungeons & Dragons Villainous Options Unearthed Arcana contain something players have been asking for a while: official rules on becoming a villainous archetype. The playtest batch contains two villainous paths: the Death Knight and the Lich.

Death Knights are fallen warriors, those who betrayed their kingdom, broke a sacred oath, or offended benevolent gods on a scale so horrendous they were cursed with undeath. Lord Soth from the Dragonlance setting is the most popular version of the archetype. 

The Lich is a D&D classic. A wizard that underwent a dark ritual, usually involving the sacrifice of hundreds of souls, to cling on to life. Most great D&D villains like Thay's Szass Tam and Vecna, The Whispered One are classic examples of this villain.

According to the playtest material, these villainous paths are tied to feats. If your character, and your DM, are okay with going down these routes, you pick the Death Knight/Lich Initiate Feat, followed by at least two of the other feats available, and finally taking the Death Knight/Lich Ascension Feat at level 12.

On paper, these Villainous Path Feats do add some layered variety, expanding on D&D 5.5e's Feats having more prerequisites. Death Knight Feats provide Death Points, which can be spent to use spells like Command and Wrathful Smite. and even summoning orbs of hellfire. Lich Feats revolve around creating a soul jar, and the ability to feed it the souls of enemies you have slain.

Naturally, if and when this playtest material is published in an official book, it will be after hearing player feedback. The survey for which will go live on April 9.

Ever since he was small, Tyler Chancey has had a deep, abiding love for video games and a tendency to think and overanalyze everything he enjoyed. This… More about Tyler



https://techraptor.net/tabletop/news/new-dungeons-dragons-villainous-options-unearthed-arcana-lets-you-be-bad-guy
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot
